{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider a line-search Newton method in which the step length \\(\\alpha_k\\) is chosen as the exact minimizer of the merit function \\(f(\\cdot)\\), that is,\n\\[\n\\alpha_k = \\arg\\min_{\\alpha} f\\bigl(x_k - \\alpha J_k^{-1} r_k\\bigr).\n\\]\nShow that, if \\(J(x)\\) is nonsingular at the solution \\(x^*\\), then \\(\\alpha_k \\to 1\\) as \\(x_k \\to x^*\\)."}
